steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Jack sarcastically says Bartley would carry a leaky pail on his head instead of a hat, just to have something to complain about. This reveals Jack's view of Bartley's character — not anything about weather or hay.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Jack doubts Bartley's ability to predict weather': Jack's line is about complaining, not weather-predicting skill. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Jack thinks Bartley wants to help with the hay': nothing in the exchange suggests cooperative intent from Bartley. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Jack believes Bartley misjudges the weather': the pail-on-head image is about inventing reasons to complain, not about weather accuracy. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Jack believes Bartley is always ready to find fault': the sarcasm directly implies Bartley would manufacture a complaint even where none exists — perpetually fault-finding. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
